frameworks.name: STRIDE Threat Model
frameworks.description: Owlvex-native STRIDE reasoning profile for code scanning, evidence capture, and reporting.

profiles.title: Owlvex STRIDE Profile 2026.1
profiles.description: Formalized STRIDE profile for code-centric security analysis in AI-assisted developer workflows.

categories.name: Spoofing
categories.question: Can an attacker pretend to be another user, service, or device?

categories.name: Tampering
categories.question: Can an attacker modify data, commands, state, or workflows improperly?

categories.name: Repudiation
categories.question: Can a user perform a sensitive action without reliable traceability?
code_signals: missing audit log for privileged action

categories.name: Information Disclosure
categories.question: Can secrets or sensitive data be exposed to unauthorized parties?

categories.name: Denial of Service
categories.question: Can the code be abused to exhaust resources or degrade service?

categories.name: Elevation of Privilege
categories.question: Can an attacker gain capabilities beyond their intended authorization?
code_signals: admin-only path reachable by normal users
policy decisions made on untrusted data

severity_guidance.critical: Remote code execution, full compromise, or high-confidence compromise of privileged assets.
severity_guidance.high: Unauthorized access, sensitive data exposure, or reliable exploit path with significant impact.
severity_guidance.medium: Meaningful weakness with constrained preconditions or partial impact.
severity_guidance.info: Advisory improvement or contextual risk note.

prompt_guidance: Classify each finding into one or more STRIDE categories.
Explain the attacker path and affected trust boundary.
Prefer code-grounded evidence over generic framework summaries.
